title: EME Temple
tagline: EME Temple — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: The Dakshinamoorthy and Navgraha EME Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to god Shiva, situated in EME School's campus in Vadodara, Gujarat, India.
overview.description: The Dakshinamoorthy and Navgraha EME Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to god Shiva, situated in EME School's campus in Vadodara, Gujarat, India. It has a Navgraha temple also which is dedicated to the nine planets in Vedic astronomy. The EME Temple was built in 1966 by the Electronics & Mechanical Engineers (EME) School of Indian Army and is maintained and run by the Army in Vadodara city of Gujarat. It has a unique design inspired by Buckminster Fuller’s geodesic dome and amalgamates elements of all major religions of the world.

topPlaces.name: Vadodara Urban Development Authority
topPlaces.description: The Vadodara Urban Development Authority is a civilian government body responsible for overseeing and sanctioning construction and infrastructure development across the suburbs of the city of Vadodara, in the state of Gujarat in India. The office of VUDA is at Karelibaug Near L&T Circle(parth), Vadodara.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Baroda
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Baroda (Latin: Baroden(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Baroda in the ecclesiastical province of Gandhinagar in India.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Kohima
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Kohima (Latin: Kohimaen(sis)) is a diocese headquartered in the city of Kohima, Nagaland, in the ecclesiastical province of Imphal in India. Its territory includes the entire state of Nagaland.

topPlaces.name: Karelibaug
topPlaces.description: Karelibaug is an area in the northern part of Vadodara in the state of Gujarat. It is one of the most developed and easily approachable locality of Vadodara city.

topPlaces.name: Baroda Museum & Picture Gallery
topPlaces.description: The Baroda Museum & Picture Gallery is an archeological and natural history museum in Baroda (Vadodara), Gujarat, India. It was built in 1894, modelled after the Victoria & Albert Museum and the Science Museum in London.

topPlaces.name: Sayaji Baug
topPlaces.description: Sayaji Baug (also known as Kamati Baug) is a garden located in Vadodara, Gujarat, India. it was built by Maharaja Sayajirao Gaekwad - a great visionary ruler of Baroda.

topPlaces.name: Vadodara Bus Station
topPlaces.description: The Vadodara bus station or Vadodara Central Bus Terminal is the central bus station serving Vadodara city in Gujarat, India.

topPlaces.name: Sardar Patel Planetarium
topPlaces.description: Sardar Patel Planetarium is a planetarium in Sayajibaug, Vadodara, Gujarat, India which was inaugurated in 1976. The pyramid-like building has four floors housing an amphitheatre, a planetarium chamber, an exhibition space and an observatory.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Vadodara Urban Development Authority & around

items.note: The Vadodara Urban Development Authority is a civilian government body responsible for overseeing and sanctioning construction and infrastructure development across the suburbs of the city of Vadodara, in the state of Gujarat in India. The office of VUDA is at Karelibaug Near L&T Circle(parth), Vadodara.
The Roman Catholic Diocese of Baroda (Latin: Baroden(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Baroda in the ecclesiastical province of Gandhinagar in India.

The Roman Catholic Diocese of Kohima (Latin: Kohimaen(sis)) is a diocese headquartered in the city of Kohima, Nagaland, in the ecclesiastical province of Imphal in India. Its territory includes the entire state of Nagaland.
Karelibaug is an area in the northern part of Vadodara in the state of Gujarat. It is one of the most developed and easily approachable locality of Vadodara city.

The Baroda Museum & Picture Gallery is an archeological and natural history museum in Baroda (Vadodara), Gujarat, India. It was built in 1894, modelled after the Victoria & Albert Museum and the Science Museum in London.
Sayaji Baug (also known as Kamati Baug) is a garden located in Vadodara, Gujarat, India. it was built by Maharaja Sayajirao Gaekwad - a great visionary ruler of Baroda.
The Vadodara bus station or Vadodara Central Bus Terminal is the central bus station serving Vadodara city in Gujarat, India.

Sardar Patel Planetarium is a planetarium in Sayajibaug, Vadodara, Gujarat, India which was inaugurated in 1976. The pyramid-like building has four floors housing an amphitheatre, a planetarium chamber, an exhibition space and an observatory.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit EME Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit EME Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach EME Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Vadodara Airport (4 km) and the nearest railway station is Vadodara Junction (3 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for EME Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of EME Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in EME Temple?
faq.a: Stays in EME Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in EME Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vadodara Urban Development Authority, Diocese of Baroda, Diocese of Kohima, Karelibaug, Baroda Museum & Picture Gallery.
